assumptions: No prior Paddle account configuration or successful use exists.
The developer has the browser and command-line tools listed by the selected path.

developer_goal: A Paddle.js overlay checkout opens in sandbox and completes a test payment for a configured price.

first_success.outcome: A Paddle.js overlay checkout opens in sandbox and completes a test payment for a configured price.
first_success.observable_signal: The overlay displays the successful checkout state after valid sandbox payment details are submitted.

route_selection.surface: Paddle.js overlay checkout quickstart (sandbox test payment)
route_selection.rule: Prefer the official hosted quickstart and cURL when documented; stop at the first observable successful happy-path result.
route_selection.selected: Paddle.js overlay checkout quickstart (sandbox test payment)

candidates.name: Paddle.js overlay checkout quickstart (sandbox test payment)
candidates.reason: It is the specified official path to the first-success boundary.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to Paddle Sandbox.
Create a product and price, then create or copy a sandbox client-side token.
required_path.observable_result: A sandbox price ID and client-side token are available.

required_path.action: Load Paddle.js on a page, initialize it for sandbox with the client-side token, and configure an overlay checkout for the price.
required_path.observable_result: The page can open a Paddle overlay for the configured item.

required_path.action: Open the overlay and submit the documented sandbox test payment details.
Confirm the checkout success state in the overlay.
required_path.observable_result: The test payment completion state is visible.

prerequisites.description: A web page capable of loading Paddle.js.

external_gates.description: The client-side token and price must belong to the sandbox environment.
Paddle.js must be loaded and initialized on a browser page in an allowed environment.

platform_outcomes.description: A Paddle.js overlay checkout opens in sandbox and completes a test payment for a configured price.

excluded.reason: This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.

sources.title: Take a test payment
evidence_supported: A Paddle.js overlay checkout opens in sandbox and completes a test payment for a configured price.
The overlay displays the successful checkout state after valid sandbox payment details are submitted.

uncertainties.question: Does fresh-account UI wording differ from the documented Take a test payment path?
uncertainties.impact: A human should judge minor navigation differences without moving the first-success boundary.
